title: Extracellular ATP Detection of Primary Cultured Microglia
author: Qiang Chen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.i9tch6n

steps.title: Lyophilized substrate solution was reconstituted using...
steps.instruction: Lyophilized substrate solution was reconstituted using the appropriate volume of buffer.

steps.title: ATP standard solution was diluted as follows
steps.instruction: ATP standard solution was diluted as follows: 1000pM, 500pM, 250pM, 125pM, 62.5pM, 31.2pM, 15.6pM, 7.8pM.

steps.title: The 384-well microplate was shaked for...
steps.instruction: The 384-well microplate was shaked for 2 minutes at 1,100 rpm.

steps.title: A multifunctional microplate reader (PerkinElmer, USA)...
steps.instruction: A multifunctional microplate reader (PerkinElmer, USA) was used to measure the luminescence of each group.

steps.title: The ATP content was calculated according...
steps.instruction: The ATP content was calculated according to a standard curve drawn by CurveExpert V1.4.

steps.title: For detection, we used 384-well opaque...
steps.instruction: For detection, we used 384-well opaque plates, with each well containing either 25 μl sample or ATP standard.

steps.title: Another 25 μl substrate solution was added to each well
steps.instruction: Another 25 μl substrate solution was added to each well.

steps.title: A vial of lyophilized ATP standard...
steps.instruction: A vial of lyophilized ATP standard solution was reconstituted with water.
